mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales signals strengthening household demand and near-term revenue growth for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value was 444926 for 2015-05-01 at the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS momentum is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns no more often than the unconditional base rate over three consecutive walk-forward years.

mechanism_narrative: An upward crossing in the federal funds rate marks the start of tighter discount-rate conditions, reducing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.10pp over 3 months or crosses above 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value was 0.12 for 2015-05-01 at the decision date
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle from the zero lower bound
failure_condition: Reject if triggered tightening observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll expansion combined with a falling unemployment rate signals broad income growth and lower recession probability, raising expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average gain exceeds 150 thousand and UNRATE falls over the same 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value was 141679 and UNRATE vintage value was 5.5 for 2015-05-01
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor trigger has a 60-day forward win rate below the unconditional win rate or a rolling 3-year Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production signals weakening real activity and operating leverage stress, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by more than 0.5% over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value was 105.1018 for 2015-05-01 at the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO declines are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if average triggered returns are not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A positive breakout in housing starts signals stronger construction activity, durable-goods demand, and household balance-sheet confidence, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 10% above its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value was 1135 for 2015-04-01 at the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakout observations do not deliver a higher 60-day mean S&P 500 return than non-breakout observations in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI while the policy rate remains near zero signals inflation pressure that tightens expected real financial conditions through future rate-hike expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.50%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value was 235.982 and FEDFUNDS was 0.12 at the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI-FEDFUNDS joint trigger has nonnegative average 20-day returns or if its downside hit rate is not above the unconditional downside frequency.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deterioration in household income and recession risk, causing near-term risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage value was 5.5 for 2015-05-01 at the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns at or above the unconditional positive-return frequency.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing real GDP growth when unemployment is already low signals late-cycle demand exhaustion rather than spare-capacity recovery, lowering forward earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth falls below 1% while UNRATE is below 6%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage value was 16264.1 for 2015-01-01 and UNRATE was 5.5 for 2015-05-01
regime_hint: late-cycle growth slowdown with low unemployment
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1 and UNRATE trigger produces positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or no underperformance versus baseline.
